title: Siderocarpos flexicaulis
taxonomy: Siderocarpos flexicaulis is classified within the family Mimosoideae, which is a subfamily of the legume family Fabaceae. It belongs to the order Fabales and the clade Rosid. This plant is native to the tropical regions of the Americas and is characterized by its unique flexibility. Within the genus Siderocarpos, S. flexicaulis is the only species recognized. The classification of S. flexicaulis reflects its close relationship with other legume species.

intro: Siderocarpos flexicaulis, also known as the "Iron Tree", is a plant species in the family Fabaceae, characterized by its unique ability to accumulate iron in its leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to reduce oxidative stress, inflammation, and improve iron deficiency anemia. The bioactive constituents of Siderocarpos flexicaulis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is typically prepared as a tea or capsule, and is also used to make a tonic, which is believed to support immune function and reduce fatigue.
